free_text: Elastic scattering and fusion cross sections for
7Be,7Li+27Al systems.
Gurudas College, Kolkata ;
UGC-DAE Consortium for Scientific Research, Kolkata
Beam current was measured by an electron-
suppressed Faraday cup at a distance of 1m from the
target position.
Entry was approved by Dr. S.Verma
Compiled by Balbir Kaur, Jaspreet kaur and
Babita Sharma from NIT Jalandhar 144011.
radioactive ion beam facility using 15
UD tandem accelerator
The 7Be (T1/2 =53 days) beams produced through the
p(7Li,7Be)n reaction by bombarding a 12-mu-m
polypropylene [(CH2)n] foil mounted on a rotating
target system, with 7Li (3+) beams of Elab =21.5, 23,
and 25 MeV from a 15-UD Pelletron accelerator
High-purity (>99.99%) 27Al target, rolled down to
1-mg/cm2 thickness, was used for the measurements. The
target was mounted on a target frame of 15-mm
diameter to avoid scattering from the frame.
The large area telescopic detector
setup consisted of a delta E gas ionization chamber(IC)
with axial field geometry and two E two-dimensional(2D)
position-sensitive silicon detectors 50 mm x 50 mm
each were used.
The solid angles covered by the CsI (Tl)
detectors placed at 120, 130, 140, and 150 degrees
were 13.96, 12.26, 9.69, and 7.85 msr, respectively.
Delta E - E Si telescope
(delta E = 300 mu-m, E = 2 mm) was mounted in the
7-Be production chamber at an angle of 30 degree with
respect to the beam direction to detect the elastic
recoil protons for the purpose of normalization.
Si-SB (500 mu-m) detector for counting 7Be
particles for the purpose of normalization checks.
Data were received from Dr. K.Kalita

Table 1
BARC-TIFR Pelletron, Mumbai.
Two self supported 27Al (99.99 % pure) targets of
thicknesses 86 and 691 mu-g/cm2 were used for elastic-
scattering and fusion cross-section measurements,
respectively.
A 12C target of thickness 50 mu-g/cm2 was used for
energy calibration by use of the discrete alpha-peaks
from the 12C(7Li,4He)15N reaction.
7Li beam (From BARC-Pelletron) at lab energy=10, 13,
16, 19 and 24 MeV
A set of three deltaE - E Si detector
telescopes (10 mu-m + 300 mu-m, 35 mu-m + 2 mm,
25 mu-m + 2 mm) were used
A Si detector of 2-mm thickness was kept at 30
degrees on another movable arm to monitor elastic
scattering.

Statistical uncertainty
BARC-TIFR Pelletron, Mumbai.
The elastic-scattering angular distributions
were subjected to Optical Model (OM) analysis. The
alpha-evaporation spectra were reproduced with the
statistical model calculations, and the fusion cross
sections were extracted from them. The fusion cross
sections were also extracted by subtraction of the
integrated inelastic-scattering cross sections from
the reaction cross sections obtained from the OM fits
to the elastic-scattering data.
Table 3
BARC-TIFR Pelletron, Mumbai.
The fusion cross-sections were extracted
by subtraction of the integrated inelastic-scattering
cross sections from the reaction cross sections
obtained from the OM fits to the elastic-scattering
data.
